codeReview.summary: Token OHLCV now attempts to derive candles from live pool swaps and token info endpoints now accept live price overrides, but the committed tests do not actually prove the token detail surface uses live pricing nor that a seeded fallback path still behaves correctly when providers fail.

issues.description: The feature requirement says token detail/info surfaces should reflect live token pricing when DeFiLlama succeeds, but the token detail test still exercises only the seeded path and asserts `price_usd: 1` without mocking live pricing. That leaves `/onchain/networks/:network/tokens/:address` unproven, even though the route was changed to call `fetchLiveSimpleTokenPrice`.

The new tests cover live-success responses for token info endpoints and live-derived token OHLCV, but they do not add a companion assertion proving graceful fallback to seeded token pricing/OHLCV when DeFiLlama or The Graph returns null/errors. The handoff claims fallback behavior remains covered, yet this feature's added tests do not explicitly distinguish the fallback path as requested.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `.factory/library/architecture.md` still says all 29 onchain routes are fixture-backed and describes token OHLCV/trades as synthetic, which is no longer accurate after the live DeFiLlama/The Graph wiring reviewed here.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:24-30 states onchain routes are fixture-backed, while src/modules/onchain.ts:1241-1296 and 3174-3230 now fetch live token prices and derive token OHLCV from live pool swaps when available.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and commit `9f8ba8b`. Implementation adds live token price overrides and async token OHLCV aggregation from live pool trades, but review fails because the committed tests do not actually prove live pricing on the token detail route and do not explicitly verify seeded fallback behavior for the token-level live paths. Report written for the scrutiny validator.
